metadata.description: 50 items testing selective attention and attentional control. Each item presents 3 information sources with conflicting claims. The model must answer based ONLY on a designated source, ignoring plausible alternatives from other sources.
metadata.cognitive_mapping: Selective Attention / Attentional Control (Section 7.3.2 of DeepMind framework) — top-down control of information processing, analogous to dichotic listening paradigm (Cherry, 1953)

items.topic: Population of Riverdale

sources.label: A
sources.name: National Census Bureau (2024)
sources.text: According to the 2024 National Census, the city of Riverdale has a population of 847,000 residents, reflecting a 3.2% increase from the 2020 count.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Riverdale Tourism Board
sources.text: Riverdale, home to over 1.2 million people in its greater metropolitan area, is the region's largest urban center and a popular destination for visitors.

sources.label: C
sources.name: GlobalCities Database (2022)
sources.text: Riverdale: Population 791,500 (est. 2022). Classification: mid-size city. Growth rate: moderate. Region: Northeast.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (National Census Bureau 2024), what is the population of Riverdale?

items.topic: Median household income in Greenfield County

sources.label: A
sources.name: County Economic Report (2025)
sources.text: Greenfield County's median household income rose to $78,400 in 2024, driven by growth in the technology and healthcare sectors.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Federal Reserve Regional Data
sources.text: The median household income for Greenfield County stands at $72,100 as of the most recent Federal Reserve survey period ending Q2 2023.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Local News Analysis
sources.text: Greenfield County residents earn an average of $85,000 per household, making it one of the wealthiest counties in the state according to our analysis of public records.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Federal Reserve Regional Data), what is the median household income in Greenfield County?

items.topic: Unemployment rate in Westbrook

sources.label: A
sources.name: Bureau of Labor Statistics (Jan 2025)
sources.text: The seasonally adjusted unemployment rate for the Westbrook metropolitan area was 4.1% in January 2025, down from 4.5% the previous year.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Westbrook Chamber of Commerce
sources.text: Westbrook boasts a low unemployment rate of just 3.2%, reflecting the city's robust job market and business-friendly policies.

sources.label: C
sources.name: University Economic Research Center
sources.text: Our comprehensive analysis, which includes underemployment and discouraged workers, places Westbrook's true unemployment at 6.8%, significantly higher than official figures.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (University Economic Research Center), what is Westbrook's unemployment rate?

items.topic: Average age of residents in Millbrook Township

sources.label: A
sources.name: Township Planning Department (2024)
sources.text: The median age of Millbrook Township residents is 38.7 years, a figure that has been steadily rising as the community attracts young families while retaining its aging population.

sources.label: B
sources.name: State Demographic Office
sources.text: Millbrook Township: median age 41.2 years (2023 estimate). This places it above the state average of 39.1 years.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Real Estate Market Report
sources.text: Millbrook's population skews younger than surrounding areas, with a median age of just 35.4 years, making it attractive for first-time homebuyers.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (Township Planning Department 2024), what is the median age in Millbrook Township?

items.topic: Number of public schools in Lakewood District

sources.label: A
sources.name: State Department of Education (2024-2025)
sources.text: The Lakewood Unified School District operates 34 public schools: 18 elementary, 9 middle, and 7 high schools, serving approximately 28,000 students.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Lakewood District Website
sources.text: Our district proudly serves families through 42 school campuses, including magnet programs, alternative schools, and early learning centers alongside our traditional schools.

sources.label: C
sources.name: National School Rankings Database
sources.text: Lakewood District: 31 public schools ranked. District enrollment: 26,500. Student-teacher ratio: 22:1.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Lakewood District Website), how many school campuses does the Lakewood District have?

items.topic: Poverty rate in Cedar Rapids metropolitan area

sources.label: A
sources.name: American Community Survey (2023)
sources.text: The poverty rate in the Cedar Rapids metropolitan statistical area was 11.3% in 2023, slightly below the national average of 12.4%.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Cedar Rapids Anti-Poverty Coalition
sources.text: Our research shows that 15.7% of Cedar Rapids metro residents live below the poverty line when using the Supplemental Poverty Measure, which accounts for local cost of living.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Midwest Economic Review
sources.text: Cedar Rapids maintains one of the lowest poverty rates in the region at 9.8%, attributed to its diverse manufacturing and agricultural economy.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (American Community Survey 2023), what is the poverty rate in the Cedar Rapids metro area?

items.topic: Annual visitors to Pinecrest National Park

sources.label: A
sources.name: National Park Service Annual Report (2024)
sources.text: Pinecrest National Park recorded 2.3 million recreational visits in 2024, representing a 7% increase over the previous year and a new all-time record.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Pinecrest Tourism Association
sources.text: The park and surrounding area welcome approximately 3.5 million visitors annually when including day-trippers and those using adjacent national forest lands.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Travel Industry Analytics
sources.text: Pinecrest NP: 1.9 million visits (2024 projection based on Q1-Q3 data and seasonal modeling). Rank: 24th most-visited national park.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (Travel Industry Analytics), how many visits did Pinecrest National Park receive in 2024?

items.topic: Percentage of residents with college degrees in Fairhaven

sources.label: A
sources.name: Census Quick Facts (2024)
sources.text: In Fairhaven, 42.1% of adults aged 25 and older hold a bachelor's degree or higher, compared to the national average of 33.7%.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Fairhaven Economic Development Board
sources.text: Nearly half of our adult population — 48% — holds at least a bachelor's degree, making Fairhaven one of the most educated communities in the state.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Education Research Institute
sources.text: Fairhaven's college attainment rate of 37.5% (including associate and bachelor's degrees) places it in the 72nd percentile nationally.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Fairhaven Economic Development Board), what percentage of Fairhaven's adult population holds at least a bachelor's degree?

items.topic: Crime rate in Oakville

sources.label: A
sources.name: FBI Uniform Crime Report (2024)
sources.text: Oakville reported 342 violent crimes per 100,000 residents in 2024, placing it slightly above the national average of 380 per 100,000.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Oakville Police Department Annual Summary
sources.text: Total reported crimes decreased by 12% in 2024, with 287 violent incidents per 100,000 residents, reflecting our community policing initiatives.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Public Safety Research Group
sources.text: When accounting for underreporting, Oakville's estimated violent crime rate is approximately 410 per 100,000, higher than both official city and federal figures suggest.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (FBI Uniform Crime Report 2024), what is Oakville's violent crime rate per 100,000 residents?

items.topic: Housing vacancy rate in Brookside

sources.label: A
sources.name: Housing Market Quarterly (Q4 2024)
sources.text: Brookside's residential vacancy rate dropped to 5.8% in Q4 2024, the lowest level in five years, driven by strong demand from remote workers relocating to the area.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Brookside Housing Authority
sources.text: The city's housing vacancy rate stands at 7.2%, with the majority of vacant units concentrated in older neighborhoods undergoing revitalization.

sources.label: C
sources.name: National Association of Realtors
sources.text: Brookside vacancy: 4.3% (Q3 2024). Market classification: tight. Median days on market: 18. Year-over-year price change: +6.1%.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (National Association of Realtors), what is Brookside's housing vacancy rate?

items.topic: Average depth of Lake Meridian

sources.label: A
sources.name: U.S. Geological Survey (2023)
sources.text: Lake Meridian has an average depth of 14.2 meters based on comprehensive bathymetric surveys conducted between 2021 and 2023.

sources.label: B
sources.name: State Environmental Agency
sources.text: The average depth of Lake Meridian is approximately 18 meters, though seasonal fluctuations can vary this by up to 2 meters depending on precipitation.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Lake Meridian Fishing Guide
sources.text: Lake Meridian averages about 11 meters deep, with the deepest point reaching 28 meters near the northern shore — prime territory for lake trout.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (U.S. Geological Survey 2023), what is the average depth of Lake Meridian?

items.topic: Annual CO2 emissions from Blackrock Power Plant

sources.label: A
sources.name: EPA Emissions Inventory (2024)
sources.text: The Blackrock Power Plant emitted 4.7 million metric tons of CO2 in 2024, making it the third-largest point source of greenhouse gas emissions in the state.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Blackrock Energy Company Report
sources.text: Our facility's carbon emissions totaled 3.9 million metric tons in 2024, a 15% reduction from 2020 levels, reflecting our investment in efficiency upgrades.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Environmental Defense Fund Analysis
sources.text: Independent satellite monitoring estimates Blackrock's 2024 emissions at 5.2 million metric tons of CO2 equivalent, including fugitive methane emissions not captured in facility-level reporting.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Blackrock Energy Company Report), what were the facility's carbon emissions in 2024?

items.topic: Average annual rainfall in the Thornfield Basin

sources.label: A
sources.name: National Weather Service (30-year average)
sources.text: The Thornfield Basin receives an average of 890 mm of precipitation annually based on the 1991-2020 climatological normal period.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Regional Water Authority
sources.text: Annual rainfall in the Thornfield Basin averages 1,050 mm, including snowmelt equivalent, which is critical for reservoir planning and water allocation.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Agricultural Extension Service
sources.text: Farmers in the Thornfield Basin can expect approximately 760 mm of growing-season precipitation, though total annual precipitation including winter months is somewhat higher.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (National Weather Service), what is the average annual precipitation in the Thornfield Basin?

items.topic: Mercury concentration in Clearwater River fish

sources.label: A
sources.name: FDA Fish Advisory (2024)
sources.text: Testing of largemouth bass from the Clearwater River found average mercury concentrations of 0.38 parts per million (ppm), below the FDA action level of 1.0 ppm.

sources.label: B
sources.name: State Health Department
sources.text: Mercury levels in Clearwater River fish averaged 0.52 ppm across all species tested, prompting a limited consumption advisory for pregnant women and children.

sources.label: C
sources.name: University Environmental Sciences Lab
sources.text: Our longitudinal study found mercury bioaccumulation in Clearwater River predatory fish reaching 0.71 ppm, with some individual specimens exceeding 1.0 ppm.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (University Environmental Sciences Lab), what mercury concentration was found in Clearwater River predatory fish?

items.topic: Speed of the Hayward Glacier retreat

sources.label: A
sources.name: NASA Earth Observatory (2024)
sources.text: Satellite imagery shows the Hayward Glacier has retreated at an average rate of 23 meters per year over the past decade, accelerating from 15 meters per year in the 1990s.

sources.label: B
sources.name: National Geographic Field Report
sources.text: Researchers on-site at Hayward Glacier measured a retreat of approximately 35 meters in the 2023-2024 season alone, the fastest single-year retreat on record.

sources.label: C
sources.name: International Glaciological Society
sources.text: The Hayward Glacier's mean retreat rate is 18.5 m/yr (2010-2024), consistent with regional trends for maritime glaciers at similar latitudes.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (National Geographic Field Report), how much did the Hayward Glacier retreat in the 2023-2024 season?

items.topic: Endangered species count in Redwood Preserve

sources.label: A
sources.name: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (2024)
sources.text: The Redwood Preserve harbors 12 federally listed endangered species, including 4 mammals, 3 birds, 2 amphibians, and 3 plant species.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Preserve Management Plan
sources.text: Our biologists have identified 17 species of conservation concern within the Redwood Preserve boundaries, of which 8 are critically endangered.

sources.label: C
sources.name: State Natural Heritage Program
sources.text: The Redwood Preserve supports 9 state-listed endangered species, though this count does not include 3 additional candidate species currently under review.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (State Natural Heritage Program), how many endangered species are in the Redwood Preserve?

items.topic: Soil pH in the Greenvalley Agricultural Zone

sources.label: A
sources.name: USDA Soil Survey (2024)
sources.text: Soils in the Greenvalley Agricultural Zone have a median pH of 6.4, classified as slightly acidic and generally well-suited for a wide range of crops.

sources.label: B
sources.name: County Agricultural Extension
sources.text: Soil testing across 200 Greenvalley farms shows an average pH of 5.9, suggesting many fields would benefit from lime application to raise pH for optimal yields.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Precision Agriculture Tech Report
sources.text: Our sensor network covering 15,000 acres in Greenvalley reports a mean soil pH of 6.8, with significant variation between fields (range: 5.5 to 7.4).

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (County Agricultural Extension), what is the average soil pH in the Greenvalley Agricultural Zone?

items.topic: Wind speed at the proposed Sterling Wind Farm site

sources.label: A
sources.name: National Renewable Energy Laboratory
sources.text: Wind resource assessment for the Sterling site indicates a mean annual wind speed of 7.8 m/s at 80-meter hub height, placing it in Wind Power Class 4.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Sterling Energy Development LLC
sources.text: Our on-site meteorological tower has recorded average wind speeds of 8.5 m/s over the past 18 months, exceeding projections and confirming excellent viability.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Independent Environmental Assessment
sources.text: The Sterling site experiences average winds of 6.9 m/s at the proposed turbine height, which may be marginal for economic viability without subsidies.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (Independent Environmental Assessment), what is the average wind speed at the Sterling Wind Farm site?

items.topic: Water temperature of Silverstone Hot Springs

sources.label: A
sources.name: State Geological Survey
sources.text: The main pool at Silverstone Hot Springs maintains a year-round temperature of 42°C (108°F), fed by a geothermal source approximately 800 meters deep.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Silverstone Resort Brochure
sources.text: Our natural hot springs pools range from a soothing 38°C to an invigorating 45°C, with the signature pool maintained at a perfect 40°C for guest comfort.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Geothermal Research Institute
sources.text: Silverstone Hot Springs discharge water at 47.3°C at the source, cooling to approximately 44°C in the main bathing pool depending on ambient conditions.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (State Geological Survey), what is the temperature of the main pool at Silverstone Hot Springs?

items.topic: Earthquake magnitude near Ridgemont

sources.label: A
sources.name: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program
sources.text: A magnitude 4.7 earthquake was recorded 12 km northwest of Ridgemont at 03:42 UTC on March 5, 2025, at a depth of 8.2 km.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Local News Report
sources.text: A moderate earthquake estimated at magnitude 5.1 struck near Ridgemont early this morning, rattling dishes and triggering car alarms across the city.

sources.label: C
sources.name: European-Mediterranean Seismological Centre
sources.text: Seismic event near Ridgemont: M 4.4, depth 10 km, 2025-03-05 03:42:17 UTC. Felt reports received from within a 60 km radius.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Local News Report), what was the estimated magnitude of the earthquake near Ridgemont?

items.topic: Founding year of Hartfield University

sources.label: A
sources.name: University Archives
sources.text: Hartfield University was chartered on September 15, 1847, when the state legislature approved the establishment of a liberal arts college in the Hartfield township.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Encyclopedia of American Universities
sources.text: Hartfield University, founded 1852, is a private institution known for its programs in engineering and natural sciences. Current enrollment: 8,200.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Hartfield Alumni Magazine
sources.text: Our university traces its origins to 1843, when Reverend Thomas Hartfield first proposed establishing an institution of higher learning in the valley.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (University Archives), when was Hartfield University chartered?

items.topic: Casualties in the Battle of Thornton Pass

sources.label: A
sources.name: National Military Archives
sources.text: The Battle of Thornton Pass (1863) resulted in 2,340 combined casualties: 890 Union and 1,450 Confederate, making it one of the bloodiest engagements in the Western Theater.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Civil War Historical Society
sources.text: Losses at Thornton Pass totaled approximately 3,100, with the Confederate forces suffering disproportionately due to their exposed position on the eastern slope.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Regional History Textbook
sources.text: The engagement at Thornton Pass saw roughly 1,800 casualties on both sides, though incomplete records from the Confederate side make precise counts difficult.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (Regional History Textbook), approximately how many casualties occurred at the Battle of Thornton Pass?

items.topic: Date of the Millerton Fire

sources.label: A
sources.name: City of Millerton Historical Records
sources.text: The Great Millerton Fire broke out on the evening of October 8, 1871, destroying 47 city blocks and leaving over 12,000 residents homeless.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Fire Prevention Monthly
sources.text: The catastrophic Millerton blaze of October 12, 1871, is studied in fire science curricula as an example of how dry conditions and wooden construction created devastating urban fires.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Insurance Industry Historical Database
sources.text: Millerton Fire (1871-10-09): insured losses equivalent to $42 million in current dollars. 1,200 structures destroyed. Origin: undetermined.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Fire Prevention Monthly), on what date did the Millerton Fire occur?

items.topic: Length of the Grandview Canal

sources.label: A
sources.name: Army Corps of Engineers Records
sources.text: The Grandview Canal, completed in 1825, stretches 584 km from Lake Morton to the Easton River, with 72 locks managing an elevation change of 174 meters.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Canal Heritage Foundation
sources.text: At 627 kilometers, the Grandview Canal was the longest artificial waterway in North America when it opened, a feat of engineering that took 8 years to complete.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Transportation History Encyclopedia
sources.text: Grandview Canal: length 551 km (navigable portion), total length including feeder channels approximately 690 km. Operational period: 1825-1917.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (Army Corps of Engineers Records), how long is the Grandview Canal?

items.topic: Year the Ridgefield Bridge was completed

sources.label: A
sources.name: State Department of Transportation
sources.text: The Ridgefield Bridge opened to traffic on November 3, 1937, after four years of construction that employed over 2,000 workers during the Great Depression.

sources.label: B
sources.name: American Society of Civil Engineers
sources.text: The Ridgefield Bridge (completed 1935) is a notable example of Art Deco engineering. Its main span of 520 meters was the longest suspension bridge west of the Mississippi at the time.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Local Heritage Walking Tour Guide
sources.text: Construction of the Ridgefield Bridge began in 1931 and was completed in 1939, though the bridge had been carrying limited traffic since late 1937.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (Local Heritage Walking Tour Guide), when was the Ridgefield Bridge completed?

items.topic: Population of Goldfield during the mining boom

sources.label: A
sources.name: State Historical Society
sources.text: At its peak in 1906, Goldfield had an estimated population of 20,000, making it the largest city in the state at the time.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Mining History Quarterly
sources.text: Goldfield's population surged to approximately 30,000 during the height of the gold rush in 1905-1907, though census records from the era are notoriously unreliable.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Goldfield Museum Exhibit
sources.text: Our town grew from a few hundred prospectors in 1902 to a bustling city of 15,000 by 1906, complete with hotels, saloons, and three newspapers.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Mining History Quarterly), what was Goldfield's peak population?

items.topic: Number of delegates at the 1912 Westfield Convention

sources.label: A
sources.name: Convention Proceedings (Official Record)
sources.text: The 1912 Westfield Convention was attended by 847 registered delegates from 38 states, who deliberated over the course of five days on the proposed constitutional amendments.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Political History of the Progressive Era
sources.text: Approximately 1,100 delegates and alternates gathered in Westfield in the summer of 1912, representing a broad coalition of reform-minded organizations.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Westfield Gazette Archive (July 1912)
sources.text: Over 600 delegates from across the nation have arrived in our city for what promises to be a historic convention, with more expected before proceedings begin tomorrow.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (Convention Proceedings), how many registered delegates attended the 1912 Westfield Convention?

items.topic: Cost of the Elmridge Aqueduct project

sources.label: A
sources.name: Public Works Department Records
sources.text: The Elmridge Aqueduct project was completed at a total cost of $14.3 million, $2.1 million over the original budget, due to unexpected geological challenges.

sources.label: B
sources.name: City Council Meeting Minutes (1928)
sources.text: The final appropriation for the Elmridge Aqueduct stands at $12.2 million, as approved by the council in a special session. Cost overruns are being investigated.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Engineering News-Record (1929 retrospective)
sources.text: The Elmridge Aqueduct, built for approximately $16 million including land acquisition and legal costs, ranks among the most expensive municipal water projects of the decade.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (Engineering News-Record), what was the total cost of the Elmridge Aqueduct?

items.topic: Height of the Centennial Monument

sources.label: A
sources.name: National Register of Historic Places
sources.text: The Centennial Monument stands 67.1 meters (220 feet) tall and was dedicated on July 4, 1876, to commemorate the nation's 100th anniversary.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Architecture Guide to the Capital
sources.text: Rising 72 meters above its granite base, the Centennial Monument is the tallest structure in the historic district and a prominent feature of the city skyline.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Monument Restoration Project Report
sources.text: The monument's total height including its copper finial is 69.8 meters. The structural survey found the limestone facade in fair condition, requiring restoration within 5 years.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (National Register of Historic Places), how tall is the Centennial Monument?

items.topic: Duration of the Silvertown textile workers' strike

sources.label: A
sources.name: Labor History Archives
sources.text: The Silvertown textile workers' strike lasted 74 days, from March 12 to May 24, 1934, involving approximately 4,500 workers from three mills.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Silvertown Heritage Center
sources.text: Workers walked off the job in mid-March 1934 and did not return until early June, enduring nearly three months of hardship before a settlement was reached.

sources.label: C
sources.name: American Labor Movement Encyclopedia
sources.text: The Silvertown Strike (1934): Duration 58 days. Workers: 3,800. Outcome: partial victory (wage increase of 12%, no union recognition).

items.question: Based ONLY on Source C (American Labor Movement Encyclopedia), how long did the Silvertown textile workers' strike last?

items.topic: Battery life of the Zenith X500 laptop

sources.label: A
sources.name: Zenith Official Specifications
sources.text: The Zenith X500 features a 72Wh battery delivering up to 14 hours of battery life under typical usage conditions (web browsing, document editing at 50% brightness).

sources.label: B
sources.name: TechReview Magazine
sources.text: In our standardized battery test, the Zenith X500 lasted 10 hours and 42 minutes, which is respectable but falls short of the manufacturer's claims.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Consumer Reports
sources.text: Battery life: 12.5 hours (CR test protocol). The Zenith X500 outlasted most competitors in its price range by approximately 2 hours.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (TechReview Magazine), how long did the Zenith X500's battery last in testing?

items.topic: Weight of the Alpine 3000 hiking boot

sources.label: A

sources.text: The Alpine 3000 hiking boot weighs 680 grams per boot (men's size 10), featuring a Vibram outsole, waterproof membrane, and full-grain leather upper.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Backpacker Magazine
sources.text: At just under 650 grams, the Alpine 3000 strikes a good balance between durability and weight for multi-day backpacking trips.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source A (Alpine Gear Catalog), how much does the Alpine 3000 hiking boot weigh?

sources.text: The all-new Meridian EV sport sedan delivers 485 horsepower and 520 lb-ft of torque from its dual-motor all-wheel-drive system, with a 0-60 mph time of 3.8 seconds.

sources.text: Meridian EV Sport Sedan: 510 hp (combined system output), 495 lb-ft torque, 310-mile range (EPA est.), 800V architecture.

items.topic: Storage capacity of the CloudVault NAS

sources.text: The CloudVault NAS supports up to 64 TB of raw storage across 4 drive bays, with hardware RAID for data protection and a 2.5GbE network interface.

items.topic: Noise level of the WhisperCool air conditioner

sources.label: A
sources.name: WhisperCool Product Specifications
sources.text: The WhisperCool 12000 operates at an ultra-quiet 42 dB on its lowest setting, comparable to a quiet library, with maximum output reaching 56 dB.

sources.label: B

sources.text: We measured the WhisperCool at 48 dB on low and 61 dB at maximum cooling — quieter than average but not as silent as marketing suggests.

items.topic: Maximum fine for noise violations in Elmwood

sources.label: A
sources.name: Elmwood Municipal Code §14.3
sources.text: Violations of the noise ordinance shall be punishable by a fine of not less than $250 and not more than $1,000 for each offense, with repeat violations subject to doubled penalties.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Elmwood Police Department FAQ
sources.text: Noise violations typically result in a warning for first offenses. If a citation is issued, fines range from $150 to $500 depending on the severity and time of day.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Tenant Rights Guide for Elmwood
sources.text: Landlords who fail to address persistent noise issues may face fines up to $2,500 per violation under the city's habitability standards, separate from the noise ordinance.

items.topic: Minimum age for employment in Westfield County

sources.label: A
sources.name: State Labor Code §2101
sources.text: No minor under the age of 14 shall be employed in any occupation except in agriculture during non-school hours, with written parental consent required.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Westfield County Youth Employment Guide
sources.text: Teens aged 15 and older may apply for work permits through the county clerk's office. Those aged 15-17 are subject to restrictions on hours and types of work.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Federal Fair Labor Standards Act Summary
sources.text: Under federal law, the minimum age for most non-agricultural employment is 14, though states may set higher minimums. Hazardous work requires a minimum age of 18.

items.topic: Statute of limitations for personal injury in the state

sources.label: A
sources.name: State Civil Code §335.1
sources.text: An action for injury to person shall be commenced within 2 years from the date of injury, except as otherwise provided in Sections 340-342.

sources.label: B
sources.name: State Bar Association Guide
sources.text: The statute of limitations for personal injury claims is generally 3 years from the date of the incident, though exceptions exist for medical malpractice (1 year) and government claims (6 months).

sources.label: C
sources.name: FindLaw State Summary
sources.text: Personal injury statute of limitations: 2 years. Property damage: 3 years. Fraud: 3 years. Written contracts: 4 years. Oral contracts: 2 years.

items.topic: Building height restriction in the Historic District

items.topic: Water usage restriction during drought Stage 2

sources.label: A
sources.name: City Water Conservation Ordinance
sources.text: During Stage 2 drought conditions, outdoor watering is restricted to two days per week (Tuesday and Saturday) between the hours of 6:00 PM and 10:00 AM only.

sources.label: B
sources.name: Water Utility Customer Notice
sources.text: Stage 2 restrictions now in effect: outdoor irrigation limited to 3 days per week. Odd-numbered addresses water on Mon/Wed/Fri; even-numbered on Tue/Thu/Sat.

sources.label: C
sources.name: Local News Coverage
sources.text: The city has moved to Stage 2 drought restrictions, meaning residents can only water their lawns once per week, with fines of up to $200 for violations.

items.question: Based ONLY on Source B (Water Utility Customer Notice), how many days per week can residents water outdoors during Stage 2 drought restrictions?
items.answer_aliases: 3 days per week|3 days|three days per week|3
